MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Nikolaus W. Reed, Esq. Law Office of Nikolaus Reed (415) 940-7766 Nik@nwrlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff, MAJED SHAIR
BRYAN CARNEY, ESQ. KENNEDY & SOUZA, APC bcarney@kennedysouza.com (858) 267-4127 Attorneys for Defendants, R BAR, INC. and LINDSAY PRZYBYL

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT R BAR, INC., AND LINDSAY PRZYBYL's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants' motion to strike the complaint's punitive damage allegations is denied. Plaintiff pleads he was drinking at defendant bar when the bar's defendant manager objected to his jukebox selection, saying: "We don't play that ghetto music here." Plaintiff noted he had paid for the selection and the manager ordered him to leave. Plaintiff asked for his credit card and a refund for his partially consumed drink. He was then accosted by three men who the manager yelled to "get him." The three slammed plaintiff to the floor, stomped him and jabbed fingers into his eyes, causing "permanent vision damage, facial nerve damage, breathing issues and facial/nose disfigurement." These allegations suffice to support punitive damages at the pleading stage (Civ. Code 3294); the facts can be developed in discovery.

order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
